channel.title: devin.ai.blog
channel.description: Latest posts from https://cognition.ai/blog/1. follow.is: None

item.title: Devin crosses the pond: Cognition opens its London office
item.description: January 28, 2026The Cognition TeamDevin crosses the pond: Cognition opens its London officeCognition expands to Europe and opens a London office.

item.title: Cognizant partners with Cognition to scale Devin and Windsurf across its engineering organization and global clients
item.description: January 28, 2026The Cognition TeamCognizant partners with Cognition to scale Devin and Windsurf across its engineering organization and global clientsCognizant has partnered with Cognition to deploy Devin and Windsurf across its engineering teams and customer base.

item.title: Agent Trace: Capturing the Context Graph of Code
item.description: January 29, 2026The Cognition TeamAgent Trace: Capturing the Context Graph of CodeWe’re excited to join in Cursor, Cloudflare, Vercel, git-ai, OpenCode and others in support of [Agent Trace](https://agent-trace.dev/). As described in the spec, Agent Trace is an open, vendor-neutral spec for recording AI contributions alongside human authorship in version-controlled codebases.

item.title: Closing the Agent Loop: Devin Autofixes Review Comments
item.description: February 10, 2026The Cognition TeamClosing the Agent Loop: Devin Autofixes Review CommentsWe built a feature that massively increased our internal token spend on Devin. But our PRs are now much more free of bugs and we can't go back.

item.title: Introducing Devin 2.2
item.description: February 24, 2026The Cognition TeamIntroducing Devin 2.2Today we’re releasing Devin 2.2, the most important update to Devin since launch.

item.title: Introducing Cognition for Government
item.description: February 25, 2026The Cognition TeamIntroducing Cognition for GovernmentToday, we launch Cognition for Government to modernize America’s critical infrastructure with AI software engineering.

item.title: How Cognition Uses Devin to Build Devin
item.description: February 27, 2026The Cognition TeamHow Cognition Uses Devin to Build DevinDevin is a cloud agent platform for engineering teams. You work with it like a teammate — give it tasks, review its PRs, and let it handle your backlog. Here's how we use it to build Devin itself.

item.title: An Early Preview of SWE-1.6 and Research Update
item.description: March 1, 2026Carlo Baronio, Ben Pan, Sam Lee, Eric Lu, Steven Cao, Rohan Choudhury, Adam Zweiger, Ray Wang, Gary Chang, Silas AlbertiAn Early Preview of SWE-1.6 and Research UpdateWe are sharing an early preview of our ongoing SWE-1.6 training run.

item.title: Devin can now Manage Devins
item.description: March 19, 2026The Cognition TeamDevin can now Manage DevinsDevin can now break down large tasks and delegate them to a team of managed Devins, with each running in its own isolated VM in parallel.

item.title: Devin can now Schedule Devins
item.description: March 20, 2026The Cognition TeamDevin can now Schedule DevinsDevin can now schedule its own recurring sessions. Run a task once, and if it goes well, tell Devin to keep doing it. It maintains state between runs, so each session picks up where the last one left off.

item.title: Introducing SWE 1.6: Improving Model UX
item.description: April 7, 2026Rohan Choudhury, Carlo Baronio, Ben Pan, Sam Lee, Eric Lu, Steven Cao, Joe Li, Andrew Wang, Adam Zweiger, Ray Wang, Gary Chang, Silas AlbertiIntroducing SWE 1.6: Improving Model UXWe're releasing SWE-1.6, our latest model optimized for both intelligence and model UX.

item.title: How Devin Is Modernizing COBOL at Fortune 500 Companies
item.description: April 8, 2026The Cognition TeamHow Devin Is Modernizing COBOL at Fortune 500 CompaniesWe wrote up why COBOL is so hard for agents, what it takes to get it right, and where Devin is delivering today.

item.title: Launching in Japan with Takumi Masai
item.description: April 9, 2026The Cognition TeamLaunching in Japan with Takumi MasaiToday we're launching Cognition Japan, our first expansion into Asia, to partner with Japanese enterprises ready to transform how software gets built.

item.title: New self-serve plans for Devin
item.description: April 14, 2026The Cognition TeamNew self-serve plans for DevinWe’re retiring the old Core and Team plans and introducing a new lineup: Free, Pro, Max, Teams, and Enterprise. We’re also beginning to charge for products that have been free until now, including Ask Devin and Devin Review.

item.title: Introducing SWE-Check: 10x Faster Bug Detection
item.description: April 14, 2026Raymond Feng¹, Jeffrey Ling², Rhythm Garg¹, Moritz Stephan² (¹ Applied Compute, ² Cognition AI)Introducing SWE-Check: 10x Faster Bug DetectionSmaller specialized models can match or beat frontier generalists on the tasks they're trained for. Working with Applied Compute, we RL-trained SWE-check, a bug detection model that matches Opus 4.6 on our internal evals while running ~10x faster.

item.title: Devin in Windsurf
item.description: April 15, 2026The Cognition TeamDevin in WindsurfLocal agents make you faster. Cloud agents do the work while you're not there.

item.title: Multi-Agents: What's Actually Working
item.description: April 22, 2026Walden YanMulti-Agents: What's Actually Working10 months ago I argued against building multi-agent systems. Today, a narrower class works, where agents contribute intelligence while writes stay single-threaded. I want to share what we've learned building them at Cognition.

item.title: What We Learned Building Cloud Agents
item.description: April 23, 2026The Cognition TeamWhat We Learned Building Cloud AgentsBuilding cloud agents is difficult. We share some learnings from building Devin.

item.title: Devin for Terminal: Start Local, Hand Off to the Cloud
item.description: April 27, 2026The Cognition TeamDevin for Terminal: Start Local, Hand Off to the CloudStart a Devin session in your terminal. When the work outgrows your laptop, hand the same session off to the cloud and keep going.

item.title: Engineering in the fast lane: Mercedes-Benz partners with Cognition
item.description: April 27, 2026The Cognition TeamEngineering in the fast lane: Mercedes-Benz partners with CognitionMercedes-Benz is deploying Devin and Windsurf across its global engineering organization, starting with legacy modernization, cloud-native development, and logistics.

item.title: Devin heads east: Cognition opens its Singapore APAC headquarters
item.description: April 30, 2026The Cognition TeamDevin heads east: Cognition opens its Singapore APAC headquartersCognition is expanding its APAC presence from Singapore, where enterprise demand, developer talent, and national AI adoption are converging. With Richard Spence leading the region, we’re helping teams across Asia-Pacific bring autonomous software engineering into production.
